content.problemStatement:
Stack has a positive integer P(P>1)P(P > 1)P(P>1).

He starts a game where his initial score is 000.

In the ithi^{th}ith turn, with a probability of 12\frac{1}{2}21â€‹, Stack adds 1Piâˆ’1\frac{1}{P^{i-1}}Piâˆ’11â€‹ to his score.

Stack stops after NNN turns.

Let SiS_iSiâ€‹ be his score after iii turns and F(i)F(i)F(i) denote the expected value of Si2S_i^2Si2â€‹.

For each integer i(1â‰¤iâ‰¤N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1â‰¤iâ‰¤N), find the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format
The only line of input contains two space-separated positive integers NNN and PPP, as mentioned in the statement.



Output Format
Print NNN space-separated integers. For each integer i(1â‰¤iâ‰¤N)i(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1â‰¤iâ‰¤N), output the value of F(i)F(i)F(i) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

In other words, output such an integer zzz that 0â‰¤z<M0 \leq z < M0â‰¤z<M and zâ‹…yâ‰¡x(modM)z \cdot y \equiv x \pmod{M}zâ‹…yâ‰¡x(modM).
